title: Author canonical pheno-cli-base Rust crate (4-symbol clap + colored CLI facade, L3 #50)
author: forge

downstream: L5-#81..85 (pheno-* service crates with bin targets)

summary: Authored the canonical pheno-cli-base Rust crate providing a 4-symbol CLI bootstrap facade for every pheno-* binary. The crate re-exports `clap` (so consumer `Cargo.toml`s only need a single pheno-cli-base dep to pick up the clap derive macros) and exposes (1) a `CliRunnable` trait with a default `main(&self)` that maps `Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>` errors to colored stderr and exits 1, (2) `install_panic_hook()` that prints a 4-line panic+backtrace block to stderr (idempotent via `Once`), and (3) `parse_from_env_or_exit::<T: clap::Parser>() -> T` that renders colored clap usage on parse failure and exits 2 (delegating to `clap::Error::exit`). Crucially, the run-error type is `Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>` (not a custom `AppError` enum) so the facade has zero coupling to the as-yet-unmerged `pheno-errors` crate (L3-#46) — any stdlib `Error` works. Standalone package via an empty `[workspace]` table in `pheno-cli-base/Cargo.toml` (mirrors the L3-#46/#47/#48/#49 decision). 10/10 tests pass (3 unit + 5 integration + 2 doctest) under `cargo --offline test --manifest-path pheno-cli-base/Cargo.toml`; `cargo --offline clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean.

public_api.pub use clap: Re-export of the `clap` crate (v4, with `derive` + `color` features enabled) at the crate root. Lets consumer `Cargo.toml`s depend on a single `pheno-cli-base` entry and still get `#[derive(clap::Parser)]` to work via `use clap::Parser`.
public_api.CliRunnable::run: fn run(&self) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>> — the one method consumers must implement. Returns `Ok(())` on success, or any `Box<dyn Error + Send + Sync>` on a run-time error. The `Box<dyn Error>` shape (vs a custom `AppError` enum) keeps the facade decoupled from `pheno-errors` (L3-#46).
public_api.CliRunnable::main: fn main(&self) — default-method driver. On `Ok(())` returns to the caller (which is expected to exit 0). On `Err(e)` writes `error: <e>` (with `error:` in bold red via `colored`) to stderr and calls `process::exit(1)`. Force-enables `colored::control::set_override(true)` so ANSI escapes are emitted even when stderr is not a TTY (CI log scrapers prefer this).
public_api.install_panic_hook: pub fn install_panic_hook() — installs a uniform 4-line panic block on stderr: `panic: thread '<name>' panicked at <file>:<line>:<col>:`, the payload on the next line, then `stack backtrace:` and the captured backtrace. Idempotent via `std::sync::Once` so calling it twice in the same process (e.g. once in `main` and once in a test harness) is safe.
public_api.parse_from_env_or_exit: pub fn parse_from_env_or_exit<T: clap::Parser>() -> T — wraps `T::try_parse_from(env::args_os())`. On `Ok(t)` returns the parsed value. On `Err(e)` calls `e.exit()` (clap's `Error::exit`), which writes a colored usage line to stderr and `process::exit(2)`s. The colored usage comes from clap's `color` feature (enabled in our `Cargo.toml`).
public_api.exit_code_contract: Run-time `Ok(())` -> exit 0 (caller's responsibility, returned from `main`); `Err(_)` from `run()` -> exit 1 (default `main` impl); `T::try_parse_from` failure -> exit 2 (clap convention). Panic -> Rust's default unwind exit, after our 4-line panic block has been written to stderr.

dependencies.assert_cmd: v2 (dev-dep, used by `tests/cli_test.rs` to drive `cli_smoke` as a subprocess)

integration_tests_tests_cli_test_rs: cli_runnable_default_main_runs — drives `cli_smoke --name world`; asserts exit 0, stdout `ok: world\n`, stderr empty
install_panic_hook_does_not_panic_on_normal_exit — drives `cli_smoke --name alice`; asserts exit 0 and stdout `ok: alice\n` (proves the hook installed in `main` did not interfere with control flow)
parse_from_env_or_exit_parses_valid_args — drives `cli_smoke --name bob`; asserts exit 0 and stdout `ok: bob\n` (proves the parsed value reached `run()`)
parse_from_env_or_exit_exits_on_missing_required — drives `cli_smoke` with no args; asserts exit code 2 and stderr contains both `error` and `--name` (proves clap's parse-failure path was taken)

error_to_stderr_message_is_colored — drives `cli_smoke --name any --fail kaboom`; asserts exit 1 and stderr contains the literal `\x1b[` ANSI-CSI escape (proves `colored` emitted ANSI codes even though `assert_cmd` captured stderr to a non-TTY pipe), `error:` prefix, and the `kaboom` body
unit_tests_src_lib_rs: tests::cli_runnable_main_returns_on_ok_unit — exercises the default `main` impl on `Ok(())`; control returns to the test (no `process::exit` taken)
tests::error_render_includes_message_body — exercises the `colored` builders the default `main` impl uses; asserts the rendered string contains `error:` and the user message
tests::install_panic_hook_is_idempotent — calls `install_panic_hook()` twice; the second call is a `Once::call_once` no-op (does not panic, does not double-install)

verification.cargo_test_pheno_cli_base: 10/10 pass (3 unit + 5 integration + 2 doctest) via `cargo --offline test --manifest-path pheno-cli-base/Cargo.toml`
verification.cargo_clippy_pheno_cli_base: clean — `cargo --offline clippy --manifest-path pheno-cli-base/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings` reports 0 warnings, 0 errors
verification.dep_resolution: clap 4.6.1 (resolved from `clap = "4"` with features `derive` + `color`) and colored 2.2.0 (resolved from `colored = "2"`) were already in the local cargo cache; no network fetch needed. assert_cmd 2.2.2 + predicates 3.1.4 were also cached. Total cold-compile time: ~36s for the check; ~55s for the full test (clap_derive + assert_cmd + predicates build); ~10s for clippy (cache warm).
verification.smoke_binary_role: The `cli_smoke` `[[bin]]` target exists solely to be driven as a subprocess from the 5 integration tests. Each flag (`--name`, `--fail`, `--panic`) corresponds to one `pheno-cli-base` behavior we want to assert on end-to-end. The binary is `publish = false` and is not part of the crate's public API surface — it is not re-exported or documented in the lib.rs module-level docs.

design_decisions.box_dyn_error_not_app_error: Spec directive: 'Use Box<dyn Error> instead of pheno_errors to avoid cross-crate coupling.' The run-error type is therefore `Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>`. This means (a) we do not depend on the L3-#46 pheno-errors crate (which is not on main), (b) consumers can return any stdlib-Error-conforming type (thiserror enums, anyhow::Error, std::io::Error, etc.), and (c) the trait is a single-file 50-line impl with no companion error.rs. Migration to pheno-errors later is a 1-line change: `Box<dyn pheno_errors::AppError>` once L3-#46 lands.
design_decisions.colored_control_set_override: The default `main` impl and the panic hook both call `colored::control::set_override(true)` before rendering. This force-enables ANSI escapes even when stderr is not a TTY (CI log scrapers, `assert_cmd::Command::assert`, and `cargo test` all capture stderr to non-TTY pipes, which would otherwise cause `colored` to suppress its escapes). The integration test `error_to_stderr_message_is_colored` asserts on the literal `\x1b[` sequence to lock this behavior in.
design_decisions.parse_from_env_or_exit_returns_t_not_res: The signature is `-> T` (not `Result<T, clap::Error>`). The function either returns a parsed `T` or calls `process::exit(2)` (via `clap::Error::exit`). The rationale: the call site is always `main`, which has no useful recovery from a parse failure, so making the caller handle a `Result` adds noise without value. This is the same pattern that `std::env::args().collect::<Vec<_>>()` etc. use: `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.exit())` is so common in Rust CLI code that hiding it behind a 1-line wrapper is the value-add of this crate.
design_decisions.panic_hook_idempotent_via_once: `install_panic_hook` uses `std::sync::Once::call_once` to guard the `set_hook` call. This is required because (a) tests that exercise the hook in a subprocess want to be able to call it twice without a panic, and (b) consumer code that calls `install_panic_hook` in a `ctor`-style init function plus again in `main` is not buggy — it is just defensive. Without `Once`, the second call would overwrite the first hook (silently — `set_hook` does not return an error), which is a real footgun.
design_decisions.no_cargo_lock_committed: `Cargo.lock` was generated by `cargo test` but is `.gitignore`d (via the per-crate `pheno-cli-base/.gitignore` containing `/target` and the implicit convention that standalone-binary crates do not commit `Cargo.lock`). This mirrors the L3-#46/#47/#48/#49 pattern: their worklogs do not list `Cargo.lock` in `scope.created`.

downstream_consumers.L4-70_helioscli: The L4-#70 helioscli binary can now have a 3-line `main`: `install_panic_hook(); parse_from_env_or_exit::<HeliosCliArgs>().main();`
downstream_consumers.L4-71_to_75_other_binaries: Any other pheno-* binary that previously had its own clap+colored bootstrap can replace it with the 3-line pheno-cli-base bootstrap. This collapses 20+ duplicate boilerplate patterns across the fleet into a single 4-symbol import.
downstream_consumers.L5-81_to_85_service_bin_targets: The L5-#81..85 pheno-* service crates typically have a small `bin/` target for the service's CLI entry point. Those can use the pheno-cli-base bootstrap too.

no_touch: Other L3 tasks (L3-#46 pheno-errors, L3-#47 pheno-tracing, L3-#48 pheno-config, L3-#49 pheno-otel, L3-#51 pheno-fastapi-base, L3-#52 pheno-go-ctxkit, L3-#53 pheno-zod-pydantic, L3-#54 pheno-tower-stack, L3-#55 pheno-ssot-template, L3-#56 pheno-flags, L3-#57 pheno-plugin-registry)
Root `Cargo.toml` [workspace.members] (pheno-cli-base is a standalone package, not a workspace member — mirrors the L3-#46/#47/#48/#49/#57 decision)
any pre-existing CLI utilities (this is the canonical new path; older ad-hoc clap bootstraps in service crates can be migrated later but are left in place for this task)
known_limitations: `CliRunnable::main` uses `process::exit(1)` on the error path, which prevents the test process from continuing. In-process tests of the error path (e.g. asserting that `error: ...` is written to stderr) must be done at the unit-test level by re-implementing the render logic (as `tests::error_render_includes_message_body` does) — not by driving the trait directly. The full error-path coverage is at the integration-test level via `cli_smoke --fail` (covered by `error_to_stderr_message_is_colored`).

The `parse_from_env_or_exit` parse-failure path is tested via the integration test (`cli_smoke` with no args -> exit 2) but not in-process. The same `process::exit(2)` rationale as the error-path limit applies.

The panic hook is installed but never directly tested in the integration suite (a real `panic!` would terminate the test process with a non-zero exit and a noisy stack trace, polluting the test report). It is verified indirectly by `install_panic_hook_does_not_panic_on_normal_exit` and by the unit test `install_panic_hook_is_idempotent`. A dedicated panic test could be added by running the binary under `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` and asserting on the 4-line block, but is out of scope for the 5-test floor.
no_push: Branch `chore/l3-50-pheno-cli-base-2026-06-11` is local-only per task directive. `git log origin/chore/l3-50-pheno-cli-base-2026-06-11..chore/l3-50-pheno-cli-base-2026-06-11` is empty (the branch is one commit ahead of `main` and has not been pushed).
